task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the title bar, Xsheet, or timeline indicates that 'dwanko_run' is loaded.

task_completion.description: Apply a Mosaic or Pixelate effect to the character animation column
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Schematic or Xsheet shows a 'Mosaic' or 'Pixelate' effect node/column added and connected to the character animation column.

task_completion.description: Set the output format to PNG and the destination to the specified mosaic_censor directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog is visible, with the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/mosaic_censor/' (or similar relative path) and the file format set to 'png'.
A rendering progress bar is visible, or the 'Render' command is actively being executed from the OpenToonz menu.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least 10 PNG frames are generated in the output directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows at least 10 PNG files inside '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/mosaic_censor/'. If image previews are visible, the character should appear pixelated/blocky.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to apply the effect and render the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenToonz graphical interface (menus, FX schematic, output settings) rather than using terminal scripts to bypass the application.

integrity.description: The agent applied the effect within OpenToonz rather than modifying image files externally
integrity.visual_evidence: The FX schematic or Xsheet in OpenToonz clearly shows the Mosaic/Pixelate effect node actively linked to the animation column.

integrity.description: The agent configured the render output correctly rather than manually moving files after a default render
integrity.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window in OpenToonz explicitly shows the target path '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/mosaic_censor/' before rendering.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The scene file is located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The output must be saved to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/mosaic_censor' as a PNG sequence. At least 10 frames must be generated. A valid mosaic/pixelate effect will result in a blockiness score > 0.15, which visually means the character should appear noticeably pixelated/blocky in the final rendered frames.
